# Boss Raid Docs Documentation

> Raid-oriented multi-agent orchestration

Boss Raid is a raid-oriented execution platform. Mercenary sanitizes tasks, routes work to fresh HTTP providers, evaluates submissions, and splits payout equally across successful providers only.

## Getting Started

- [Introduction](/docs/getting-started/introduction): Boss Raid is the platform. Mercenary is the orchestrator agent.
- [Quick Start](/docs/getting-started/quick-start): Use this path to boot the local stack and hit the API quickly.
- [Local Development](/docs/getting-started/local-development): The default local workflow is:

## Platform

- [Product Model](/docs/platform/product-model): Boss Raid is a raid-oriented execution platform for sending one task to multiple providers and selecting valid outcomes through evaluation.
- [Architecture](/docs/platform/architecture): Boss Raid is the platform. Mercenary is the orchestrator agent inside it.
- [Raid Lifecycle](/docs/platform/raid-lifecycle): Every raid goes through the same high-level flow.
- [Providers](/docs/platform/providers): Providers are the specialist execution layer behind Boss Raid.
- [Apps And Packages](/docs/platform/apps-and-packages): The monorepo is split into public apps, service apps, and shared packages.

## API Reference

- [Overview](/docs/api-reference/overview): Boss Raid has one orchestration engine with multiple ingress surfaces.
- [Native Raid](/docs/api-reference/native-raid): Use `POST /v1/raid` as the canonical public write route.
- [Chat Completions](/docs/api-reference/chat-completions): `POST /v1/chat/completions` is the compatibility surface.
- [Providers And Registry](/docs/api-reference/providers-and-registry): Boss Raid routes work to external HTTP provider workers.
- [MCP Server](/docs/api-reference/mcp-server): Boss Raid ships a stateless MCP adapter over the HTTP API.

## Operations

- [Runtime And Environment](/docs/operations/runtime-and-environment): The standard operator loop is:
- [Persistence And State](/docs/operations/persistence-and-state): Boss Raid persists raid state and proof surfaces instead of treating orchestration as a transient chat response.
- [Settlement And Contracts](/docs/operations/settlement-and-contracts): Boss Raid has two settlement modes.
- [Pricing And Payouts](/docs/operations/pricing-and-payouts): Boss Raid does not use one global flat price.
- [Troubleshooting](/docs/operations/troubleshooting): Most common cause: the caller omitted the explicit payout budget.

## Reference

- [Examples And Payloads](/docs/reference/examples-and-payloads): The Boss Raid app repo ships a small set of source-of-truth example payloads.
- [Current Limits](/docs/reference/current-limits): This page is intentionally blunt. These are the current constraints of the platform, not aspirational marketing.
- [UI Direction](/docs/reference/ui-direction): - `apps/web` is public
- [Hackathon](/docs/reference/hackathon): - Boss Raid is the platform and raid flow
- [Synthesis Registration](/docs/reference/synthesis-registration): Registration is deferred. Keep the requirements in repo, but do not call registration until the build and submission story are ready.
